Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Depositions Of Redy Colombini And /Or The Person Most Knowledgeable From Defendant Music City Hotel, L.P., And For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-16-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF TUCK AIRE HEATING & AIR CONDITIONING CORPORATION DOING BUSINESS AS ATLAS HEATING AND VENTILATING COMPANY's Motion To Compel Depositions Of Redy Colombini And /Or The Person Most Knowledgeable From Defendant Music City Hotel, L.P., And For Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Tuck Aire Heating & Air Conditioning Corporation's unopposed motion to compel the depositions of Rudy Colombini and a person most qualified from Music City Hotel LP is granted. Colombini and Music City Hotel are ordered to appear for deposition and produce documents as required by the deposition notices at Exhibits 2 and 5 of the Sullivan Declaration within 15 days of entry of this order or at another date mutually agreed by the parties. The court finds that Music City's discovery position lacked substantial justification and orders it to pay $2475 to Tuck Aire within 15 days of entry of this order.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Request For Admissions, And Request For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-10-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing re: PLAINTIFF TUCK AIRE HEATING & AIR CONDITIONING CORPORATION, DOING BUSINESS AS ATLAS HEATING AND VENTILATING COMPANY's Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Request For Admissions, And Request For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Tuck Aire Heating & Air Conditioning Corporation's Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Request For Admissions, And Request For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions is GRANTED. Defendants shall provide code compliant responses without objections by December 31, 2024. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is GRANTED IN PART. Defendants did not act with substantial justification. Defendants shall pay to Plaintiff sanctions in the amount of $2,000.

Order signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (301/JMQ)
